tasks.spec:
You are a zero-cost mock worker staging a wire-capture probe for the fleet's swarm-allocation design council. The question under test: can OpenCode stamp a PER-INVOCATION session id into provider headers via {env:RINGER_SESSION_ID} substitution? No real model is involved anywhere — the probe points OpenCode at a local capture sink (scripts/probes/header_sink.py) and inspects exactly what reaches the wire. Boundary: you own only probe-note.md in your task directory; the executed check performs the actual probe (scripts/probes/opencode-env-session-probe.sh) and is the sole verdict. Write the marker file below.
MOCK_FILE: probe-note.md
# OpenCode env-session probe
This task's check runs the wire-capture probe: two `opencode run` invocations with different RINGER_SESSION_ID values against a local header sink; PASS requires both distinct ids to arrive as X-Session-Id headers, substituted, with wire model auto/coding.
MOCK_END
tasks.verified: the capture sink actually received two OpenCode requests whose X-Session-Id headers carry the two distinct per-invocation env values, proving per-worker session stamping is possible client-side
